results.description: User-supplied transcript variable is concatenated into SQL via string interpolation '{var}'. This allows arbitrary SQL execution from VoC text content.
results.patch_suggestion: Use parameterized query with bind variables instead of f-string interpolation.

results.description: Streamlit application has no authentication layer. All pages are accessible to anyone with the URL. Critical IDOR-type vulnerability.
results.patch_suggestion: Add streamlit-authenticator middleware or Azure AD integration before page rendering.

results.description: LLM-generated SQL is passed directly to run_query() without validation or sandboxing. Allows arbitrary SQL execution via prompt injection.
results.patch_suggestion: Wrap run_query() with allowlist of permitted operations (SELECT only) and parsed AST verification.

results.description: Connection uses shared PAT token from environment variable. No token rotation, no per-user token. Token leakage compromises all data.
results.patch_suggestion: Switch to OAuth2 device flow with per-user tokens and 1-hour expiration.

results.description: External HTTP calls accept user-supplied URL. No allowlist or IMDS metadata blocking. Possible SSRF to internal services.
results.patch_suggestion: Add URL validator with allowlist of permitted domains. Block 169.254.169.254 (AWS IMDS) explicitly.

results.description: User-supplied filename used in file path without sanitization. Limited impact due to read-only context but could expose internal paths.
results.patch_suggestion: Use os.path.basename() and validate against allowlist of allowed filenames.
